parsed.summary: Cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff spans 84 files with 6600+ additions including a deleted module (process.mjs), a new 203-line retry subsystem, a new 168-line error-dispatch layer, and heavy modifications to the core client and job-control path. The combination of module deletion, new retry semantics, and concurrent job-tracking changes creates concrete risk of runtime import crashes, retry-induced duplication of non-idempotent API calls, and silent state corruption. Too many moving parts are touching the critical submit/track/render pipeline simultaneously to ship without targeted verification.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — residual imports will crash at runtime
findings.body: The diff stat shows `scripts/lib/process.mjs` with 67 lines removed (file deleted). The companion `check-imports.mjs` was updated (5 lines changed), suggesting an import migration was attempted. However, if ANY module still statically or dynamically imports from `process.mjs`, the entire glm-companion will fail with MODULE_NOT_FOUND at startup — a silent regression that basic happy-path testing may not catch if the import path is only reached in background/wait modes or error-recovery branches.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for any remaining reference to 'process.mjs' or 'process' in import/from statements. Add a CI check that validates all import targets resolve. Specifically test the --background and --wait execution modes added in v0.4.5, since process.mjs likely provided process-management utilities those paths depend on.

findings.title: New retry.mjs (203 lines) may retry non-idempotent job-submission calls
findings.body: A brand-new retry module with 203 lines was added. The glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) and job-control path were modified in the same span. If the retry logic wraps job submission or status-mutation API calls to the GLM backend, a retried request could create duplicate jobs or advance state twice. The tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) additions suggest job state is being persisted, but there is no evidence of idempotency keys or request deduplication in the diff stat. Under network-partition or timeout conditions, this could produce duplicate submissions that the user cannot detect or cancel.
findings.recommendation: Audit every call site where retry logic is applied. Confirm that each wrapped operation is either (a) read-only, (b) idempotent by design, or (c) guarded by an idempotency key. If job submission is retried, add a pre-submit check that queries existing jobs to prevent duplicates. Add integration tests that simulate transient network failures during submission and assert exactly-once job creation.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error dispatch may silently swallow unrecognized errors
findings.body: A new 168-line error-categorization module was added alongside retry logic. Error dispatch tables are notoriously fragile: if the categorization logic falls through to a default/catch-all that treats unrecognized errors as retryable, any new error variant from the upstream API (rate-limit format changes, auth expiry payloads, new HTTP status codes) will be retried indefinitely instead of surfacing to the user. Conversely, if the default is non-retryable, new transient error types will immediately fail. Either direction is a silent-failure risk.
findings.recommendation: Verify the default/fallback branch in the error classifier. Ensure unrecognized errors are logged with full detail (status, headers, body) AND surfaced to the user — not silently retried or discarded. Add a test case for a completely unknown error shape and assert it is handled correctly. Consider adding a metric or log counter for 'unrecognized error type' to detect schema drift from the upstream API.

findings.title: Concurrent modifications to state.mjs + tracked-jobs.mjs risk race-condition corruption
findings.body: state.mjs (32 lines changed) and tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) were both modified in this span. These modules appear to manage persistent job and session state. If two concurrent invocations of the companion (e.g., a --background job and a foreground status check) both read-modify-write the same state file without file locking, the later write will silently overwrite the earlier one, losing tracked jobs. The addition of --background and --wait modes in v0.4.5 makes concurrent access a real scenario rather than theoretical.
findings.recommendation: Verify that all state-file writes use atomic write (write-to-temp + rename) and that concurrent access is either serialized via file locks (flock/lockfile) or designed to use append-only/merge semantics. Add a test that runs two concurrent state mutations and asserts both are reflected in the final state.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 — hardening changes are unverified end-to-end
findings.body: The test-automation directory contains UAT reports for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 but NOT for v0.4.6. The v0.4.6 release (the current HEAD) includes BigModel error dispatch, auto retry/backoff, and release-yml retirement — all changes to the critical request path. These are exactly the kind of changes that need end-to-end verification against a live or realistic mock backend. The absence of v0.4.6 UAT means the retry/error-handling integration has no evidence of working under real conditions.
findings.recommendation: Before shipping, execute a v0.4.6 UAT cycle covering: (1) successful submission with no errors, (2) submission during transient 5xx from upstream, (3) submission during auth failure (401/403), (4) submission during rate-limit (429), (5) concurrent foreground + background operations. Document results in test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/.

next_steps: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs\|from.*process' scripts/` to verify no dangling imports from the deleted module.
Audit every retry.mjs call site for idempotency; confirm job-submission endpoints are not blindly retried.
Verify the default branch in bigmodel-errors.mjs does not silently swallow or infinitely retry unrecognized error shapes.
Run concurrent state-mutation tests against state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs to confirm no data loss under parallel access.
Execute and document a v0.4.6 UAT cycle before promoting this cumulative release.
